title: A Multifunctional Bistable Ultrathin Composite Boom for In-Space Monitoring of Deployment Dynamics
expected_content_elements: Title, author list, affiliations (NASA and partners)
Sections on motivation, concept, design, experimental setup, and results
Figures showing boom geometry, deployment, and sensor layout
Graphs of deployment dynamics and sensor measurements
key_details: Presents a deployable ultrathin composite boom with integrated flexible electronics for CubeSats.
Describes mechanical design, bistability characteristics, and embedded sensing for deployment monitoring.
Includes experimental data and planned/actual on-orbit demonstration details.
semantic_themes: Structural health monitoring with embedded sensors
layout_features: Conference-paper style layout, possibly single-column but dense with figures.
Numerous figure callouts and references to equations or models.
images_and_diagrams_description: Contains photographs of the composite boom prototype, schematic drawings of the boom cross-section and deployment configuration, and plots of measured responses vs time.
tables_description: May include small tables summarizing material properties or test conditions, but the document is figure-heavy.
extraction_checklist: Did the extractor maintain association between figure captions and the corresponding images?
Were all section headings captured cleanly?
Were any equations or symbols (e.g., Greek letters, strain notation) preserved correctly?
Are numeric values from any tables or bullet lists correctly extracted?
Is the abstract clearly separated from the main body?
